title: [codex] fix: reject STT models on TTS endpoints
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Ears vs. Mouth
gist.blurb: We've added better signposting for our audio endpoints. If you accidentally ask a transcription model to speak, we'll gently point you to the right endpoint instead of throwing a confusing quota error.
gist.summary: Added validation to text-to-speech endpoints to reject speech-to-text models and return a helpful 400 error directing users to the correct transcription route.
gist.impact: Developers will now receive clear, actionable error messages instead of confusing upstream quota errors when accidentally using transcription models on TTS endpoints.
gist.visual_concept: The bee (Polly) acting as a helpful guide or traffic director for audio signals. A crossroads with signs pointing to a microphone (STT) and a speaker (TTS). The Nomnom creature is there, perhaps holding a microphone to its ear in confusion.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny guide hat, directing glowing audio waves at a crossroads. A wooden signpost points one way to a retro microphone and the other to a chunky pixel speaker. The round tan nomnom creature stands nearby holding a microphone to its ear, looking happily confused. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites.
